codeReview.summary: The backend adds the requested Tauri commands and gist plumbing, but two implementation gaps prevent the feature from fully matching the spec. Machine-name fallback ignores the existing computer-name config, and gist updates fail if the gist contains any extra files because reads fetch the entire raw gist instead of the required `vibogit-beacon.json` file.

issues.description: `machine_name_from_config` no longer falls back to `AppConfig.computer_name`; it only uses `sync_beacon_machine_name` and environment variables. The feature contract and handoff both say machine-name resolution should prefer the configured computer name when no beacon-specific name is set, so users with only `computerName` configured will publish an unexpected hostname/"Unknown Machine" instead of their saved machine name.

Update/validate/pull paths parse the result of `gh gist view <id> --raw`, which returns all gist files concatenated when a gist contains multiple files. Because the code never requests `vibogit-beacon.json` specifically, any user-selected gist that contains another file will produce malformed JSON and make sync/update/validation fail even though the beacon file exists. The feature explicitly requires using the `vibogit-beacon.json` file within the gist.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `tauri-fullstack` skill still mandates browser verification even for backend-only Rust features, and the worker explicitly reported deviating because no frontend/UI files changed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges[0] requests clarifying that browser verification is not required for backend-only Tauri features; `.factory/skills/tauri-fullstack/SKILL.md` section '6. Manual Verification (Browser)' currently presents browser verification as a required step.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission docs captured GitHub Gist CLI commands but not the important requirement to target a specific file when reading multi-file gists. That omission likely contributed to the backend using `gh gist view <id> --raw` without `-f vibogit-beacon.json`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission `AGENTS.md` documents `gh gist view <gist_id> --raw`; the reviewed code originally used that pattern in `read_sync_beacon_gist_raw`, which breaks when a gist has extra files.

summary: Review failed with two blocking issues: machine-name fallback does not honor the saved computer name, and gist reads are not scoped to `vibogit-beacon.json`, so multi-file gists break sync, validation, and pull. Report written for scrutiny triage.
